3191	\begin{lemma}
3192	
3193	\leanok
3194	[The slice-to-geometric isomorphism in pullback form (gap1, C, step 4')]
3195	  \label{lem:over_restrict_pullback_iso}
3196	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.overRestrictPullbackIso}
3197	  \uses{lem:over_restrict_iso, lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib,
3198	    lem:modules_pullback_mathlib}
3199	  \textit{The pullback packaging of the slice-touching bridge; the form consumed by the P1 transport.}
3200	  With \(X\) and \(U \subseteq X\) as above and \(M\) a sheaf of modules on \(X\), the transport of the
3201	  abstract Grothendieck-slice restriction \(M.\mathrm{over}\,U\) under the step-3 equivalence functor
3202	  (\cref{def:over_restrict_equiv}) is canonically isomorphic to the inverse-image (pullback) of \(M\)
3203	  along the open immersion \(U.\iota\):
3204	  \[
3205	    (\mathrm{overRestrictEquiv}\,U).\mathrm{functor}.\mathrm{obj}\,(M.\mathrm{over}\,U)
3206	      \;\cong\; (U.\iota^{*})\,M
3207	  \]
3208	  (\cref{lem:modules_pullback_mathlib}). This is the pullback packaging of the slice-touching bridge
3209	  \cref{lem:over_restrict_iso}; it is exactly the form in which a presentation of
3210	  \(M.\mathrm{over}\,U\) is transported into a presentation of the geometric pullback \(U.\iota^{*}M\),
3211	  and it is the ingredient consumed by the per-element local-tilde transport
3212	  \cref{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent}.
3213	\end{lemma}
3214	\begin{proof}
3215	  \uses{lem:over_restrict_iso, lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}
3216	  Compose the slice-to-geometric isomorphism \cref{lem:over_restrict_iso}, which identifies the
3217	  transported slice restriction with the geometric restriction
3218	  \((\mathrm{restrictFunctor}\,U.\iota).\mathrm{obj}\,M\), with the component at \(M\) of the natural
3219	  isomorphism \(\mathrm{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\,U.\iota\)
3220	  (\cref{lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}) identifying the geometric restriction with
3221	  the pullback \(U.\iota^{*}M\).
3222	\end{proof}

3229	\begin{definition}[Step-3 equivalence sends the unit module to the unit module]
3230	  \label{def:over_restrict_unit_iso}
3231	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.overRestrictUnitIso}
3232	  \uses{def:over_restrict_equiv}
3233	  \textit{The step-3 slice equivalence functor preserves the structure-sheaf (unit) module.}
3234	  Let \(U \subseteq X\) be an open subscheme. The functor of the step-3 slice
3235	  equivalence (\cref{def:over_restrict_equiv}) carries the unit
3236	  (structure-sheaf) module \(\mathbf{1}\) of the over-site
3237	  \(X.\mathrm{ringCatSheaf}.\mathrm{over}\,U\) to the unit module of
3238	  \(U.\mathrm{toScheme}.\mathrm{ringCatSheaf}\):
3239	  \[
3240	    (\mathrm{overRestrictEquiv}\,U).\mathrm{functor}.\mathrm{obj}\,
3241	      \bigl(\mathbf{1}\,(X.\mathrm{ringCatSheaf}.\mathrm{over}\,U)\bigr)
3242	      \;\cong\; \mathbf{1}\,(U.\mathrm{toScheme}.\mathrm{ringCatSheaf}) .
3243	  \]
3244	\end{definition}
3245	\begin{proof}
3246	  \uses{def:over_restrict_equiv}
3247	  By construction the functor of the equivalence is the pushforward
3248	  \(\mathrm{pushforward}\,\psi_0\) of sheaves of modules along the
3249	  over-site/open-site comparison, where \(\psi_0 = \mathrm{homMk}(\mathbf{1})\)
3250	  is the identity ring comparison. The canonical unit comparison
3251	  \(\mathrm{unitToPushforwardObjUnit}\,\psi_0 : \mathbf{1}_S \to
3252	  (\mathrm{pushforward}\,\psi_0).\mathrm{obj}\,\mathbf{1}_R\) is an isomorphism
3253	  whenever the underlying ring comparison \(\psi_0\) is one: reflecting through the
3254	  forgetful passage to presheaves (\(\mathrm{toSheaf}/\mathrm{sheafToPresheaf}\)),
3255	  its component over each object \(V\) is the functor-image of
3256	  \(\psi_0.\mathrm{app}\,V\), hence an isomorphism (this \(\mathrm{IsIso}\,\psi\)
3257	  driven criterion is supplied by the auxiliary
3258	  \(\mathrm{isIso\_unitToPushforwardObjUnit\_of\_isIso}'\)). Here
3259	  \(\psi_0 = \mathrm{homMk}(\mathbf{1})\) is the identity, so the unit comparison
3260	  is an isomorphism, and its inverse is the asserted isomorphism. (Mathlib's
3261	  isomorphism statement for \(\mathrm{unitToPushforwardObjUnit}\) instead requires
3262	  a finality hypothesis on the comparison functor; the \(\mathrm{IsIso}\,\psi\)
3263	  driven form used here is the one the slice equivalence needs.)
3264	\end{proof}

3266	\begin{definition}[Transport of a slice presentation to a geometric presentation]
3267	  \label{def:over_restrict_presentation}
3268	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.overRestrictPresentation}
3269	  \uses{def:over_restrict_unit_iso, lem:over_restrict_pullback_iso, lem:presentation_map_mathlib}
3270	  \textit{A slice presentation yields a presentation of the geometric pullback.}
3271	  Let \(U \subseteq X\) be open and \(M\) a sheaf of modules on \(X\). A
3272	  presentation of the abstract Grothendieck-slice restriction \(M.\mathrm{over}\,U\)
3273	  yields a presentation of the geometric pullback \((U.\iota^{*})\,M\):
3274	  \[
3275	    (M.\mathrm{over}\,U).\mathrm{Presentation}
3276	      \;\longrightarrow\;
3277	    \bigl((U.\iota^{*})\,M\bigr).\mathrm{Presentation} .
3278	  \]
3279	\end{definition}
3280	\begin{proof}
3281	  \uses{def:over_restrict_unit_iso, lem:over_restrict_pullback_iso, lem:presentation_map_mathlib}
3282	  Apply the functorial transport of presentations \(\mathrm{Presentation.map}\)
3283	  (\cref{lem:presentation_map_mathlib}) along the step-3 slice equivalence functor
3284	  \((\mathrm{overRestrictEquiv}\,U).\mathrm{functor}\), using
3285	  \cref{def:over_restrict_unit_iso} to identify the image of the structure-sheaf
3286	  (unit) module with the target unit module; this lands a presentation of the
3287	  transported slice module
3288	  \((\mathrm{overRestrictEquiv}\,U).\mathrm{functor}.\mathrm{obj}\,(M.\mathrm{over}\,U)\).
3289	  Finally transport across the pullback packaging isomorphism
3290	  \((\mathrm{overRestrictEquiv}\,U).\mathrm{functor}.\mathrm{obj}\,(M.\mathrm{over}\,U)
3291	  \cong (U.\iota^{*})\,M\) (\cref{lem:over_restrict_pullback_iso}) via
3292	  \(\mathrm{Presentation.ofIsIso}\), giving a presentation of the geometric
3293	  pullback \((U.\iota^{*})\,M\).
3294	\end{proof}

3296	\begin{definition}[Per-cover-member geometric presentation from quasi-coherence data]
3297	  \label{def:presentation_pullback_iota_of_quasicoherentData}
3298	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.presentationPullbackιOfQuasicoherentData}
3299	  \uses{def:over_restrict_presentation, lem:quasicoherentData_bind_mathlib}
3300	  \textit{Quasi-coherence data gives a presentation on each cover member's pullback.}
3301	  Let \(M\) be a sheaf of modules on \(X\), let \(q\) be quasi-coherence data for
3302	  \(M\), and let \(i\) be a cover index. There is a presentation of the geometric
3303	  pullback of \(M\) to the open subscheme \(q.X_i\):
3304	  \[
3305	    \bigl((q.X_i).\iota^{*}\,M\bigr).\mathrm{Presentation} .
3306	  \]
3307	\end{definition}
3308	\begin{proof}
3309	  \uses{def:over_restrict_presentation, lem:quasicoherentData_bind_mathlib}
3310	  The datum \(q\) packages, for each index \(i\), a slice presentation
3311	  \(q.\mathrm{presentation}\,i : (M.\mathrm{over}\,(q.X_i)).\mathrm{Presentation}\)
3312	  over the cover member \(q.X_i\) (the per-member presentations whose binding is
3313	  recorded by \(\mathrm{QuasicoherentData}\), \cref{lem:quasicoherentData_bind_mathlib}).
3314	  Feeding this slice presentation to \cref{def:over_restrict_presentation} at
3315	  \(U = q.X_i\) produces the desired presentation of the geometric pullback
3316	  \((q.X_i).\iota^{*}\,M\).
3317	\end{proof}

3321	\begin{lemma}[Quasi-coherent restricts to a tilde on each basic open of the cover (gap1, P1)]
3322	  \label{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent}
3323	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen}
3324	  \uses{lem:over_restrict_iso, lem:over_restrict_pullback_iso, lem:presentation_map_mathlib,
3325	    lem:quasicoherentData_bind_mathlib, lem:modules_pullback_mathlib,
3326	    lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation_mathlib, lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib,
3327	    lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData}

3329	  % per-element transport step of gap1. It replaces the former lem:exists_isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_cover,
3330	  % whose \lean{} pin named a non-existent decl (and whose Mayer--Vietoris framing is superseded by
3331	  % the section-localization descent of lem:section_localization_descent).
3332	  \textit{Per-element step: on each basic open of the refined cover, the restricted sheaf is a
3333	  geometric tilde.}
3334	  Let \(M\) be a sheaf of modules on \(\Spec R\) with quasi-coherence data \(q\), and let \(r \in R\)
3335	  be such that \(D(r) \le q.X_i\) for some member of the cover (as produced by
3336	  \cref{lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData}). Then the restricted counit is an
3337	  isomorphism,
3338	  \[
3339	    \mathrm{IsIso}\bigl((M|_{D(r)}).\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}\bigr),
3340	  \]
3341	  i.e. \(M|_{D(r)}\) is identified with the tilde \(\widetilde{N}\) of the \(R_r\)-module
3342	  \(N = \Gamma(M, D(r))\) on \(\Spec R_r \cong D(r)\).
3343	\end{lemma}
3344	\begin{proof}
3345	  \uses{lem:over_restrict_iso, lem:over_restrict_pullback_iso, lem:presentation_map_mathlib,
3346	    lem:quasicoherentData_bind_mathlib, lem:modules_pullback_mathlib,
3347	    lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation_mathlib, lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib,
3348	    def:over_restrict_presentation, def:presentation_pullback_iota_of_quasicoherentData}
3349	  The quasi-coherence datum supplies a presentation
3350	  \(q.\mathrm{presentation}\,i : (M.\mathrm{over}\,(q.X_i)).\mathrm{Presentation}\) on the slice over
3351	  \(q.X_i\). Restrict it along the inclusion of opens \(D(r) \subseteq q.X_i\) by transporting across
3352	  the iterated Grothendieck slice — the template is \(\mathrm{QuasicoherentData.bind}\)
3353	  (\cref{lem:quasicoherentData_bind_mathlib}) — using \(\mathrm{Presentation.map}\)
3354	  (\cref{lem:presentation_map_mathlib}) along the colimit-preserving slice functor; this yields a
3355	  presentation of \(M.\mathrm{over}\,(D(r))\). Pushing through the slice-to-geometric bridge
3356	  \cref{lem:over_restrict_iso} converts it into a presentation of the geometric restriction
3357	  \((\mathrm{restrictFunctor}\,(D(r)).\iota).\mathrm{obj}\,M\); then, across the affine
3358	  identification \(D(r) \cong \Spec R_r\) (\cref{lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib}, by
3359	  \(\mathrm{Presentation.map}\) along the pullback functor \cref{lem:modules_pullback_mathlib}), into
3360	  a genuine global presentation of \(M|_{D(r)}\) as a sheaf of modules on \(\Spec R_r\). A global
3361	  presentation forces the \(\widetilde{(-)}\)-counit to be an isomorphism
3362	  (\cref{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation_mathlib}), giving

3364	\end{proof}

3368	\begin{lemma}[Section-localization descent from a local-tilde cover (gap1 keystone, D)]
3369	  \label{lem:section_localization_descent}
3370	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent}
3371	  \uses{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent,
3372	    lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData, lem:existsUnique_gluing_mathlib,
3373	    lem:isLimitPullbackCone_mathlib, lem:isLocalization_flat_mathlib,
3374	    lem:isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict}
3375	  % NOTE: the pinned Lean decl isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent does NOT yet exist; it is the
3376	  % genuine new content of gap1 and is reused by gap2's section-localization characterization.
3377	  % isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_presentation does NOT close it: that lemma needs a GLOBAL
3378	  % presentation of M, whereas here only per-D(r) tilde data is available.
3379	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", \S "Sections over principal opens",
3380	  % Lemma \texttt{lemma-invert-f-sections} (read from references/stacks-properties.tex, L2152-L2170).
3381	  % Cf. [Hartshorne], II.5.3.
3382	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
3383	  % "Let $X$ be a scheme. Let $f \in \Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)$.
3384	  %  Denote $X_f \subset X$ the open where $f$ is invertible, see
3385	  %  Schemes, Lemma \ref{schemes-lemma-f-open}.
3386	  %  If $X$ is quasi-compact and quasi-separated, the canonical map
3387	  %  $$
3388	  %  \Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)_f \longrightarrow \Gamma(X_f, \mathcal{O}_X)
3389	  %  $$
3390	  %  is an isomorphism. Moreover, if $\mathcal{F}$ is a quasi-coherent
3391	  %  sheaf of $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules the map
3392	  %  $$
3393	  %  \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})_f \longrightarrow \Gamma(X_f, \mathcal{F})
3394	  %  $$
3395	  %  is an isomorphism."
3396	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", \S ``Sections over principal opens'',
3397	  Lemma \texttt{lemma-invert-f-sections}; cf.\ [Hartshorne], II.5.3.}
3398	  Let \(M\) be a quasi-coherent sheaf of modules on \(\Spec R\) (equivalently, by
3399	  \cref{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent} and
3400	  \cref{lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData}, \(M\) is locally isomorphic to a
3401	  tilde \(\widetilde{N_j}\) on the members of a finite basic-open cover \(\{D(r_j)\}\) of
3402	  \(\Spec R\)). Then for every \(f \in R\) the section restriction
3403	  \(\Gamma(M, \top) \to \Gamma(M, D(f))\) exhibits the target as the localized module
3404	  \((\mathrm{powers}\,f)^{-1}\,\Gamma(M, \top)\); that is, it is
3405	  \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}(\mathrm{powers}\,f)\) over \(R\).
3406	\end{lemma}
3407	% NOTE: the lemma STATEMENT is cited to Stacks lemma-invert-f-sections / Hartshorne II.5.3; the proof
3408	% below is a project-internal descent (finite sheaf equalizer over the cover + flatness of
3409	% localization) that deliberately does NOT route through the global affine equivalence (which is
3410	% gap1 itself), so there is no transcribed `% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF`.
3411	\begin{proof}
3412	  \uses{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent,
3413	    lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData, lem:existsUnique_gluing_mathlib,
3414	    lem:isLimitPullbackCone_mathlib, lem:isLocalization_flat_mathlib,
3415	    lem:isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict}
3416	  Refine the quasi-coherence cover to a finite basic-open cover \(\{D(r_j)\}_{j=1}^m\) of \(\Spec R\)
3417	  with each \(D(r_j) \le q.X_{i(j)}\)
3418	  (\cref{lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData}). On each \(D(r_j)\) the restricted
3419	  counit is an isomorphism (\cref{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent}), so
3420	  \(M|_{D(r_j)} \cong \widetilde{N_j}\) with \(N_j = \Gamma(M, D(r_j))\) a module over \(R_{r_j}\),
3421	  and the basic-open sections of \(M\) below \(D(r_j)\) are the corresponding module localizations
3422	  (\cref{lem:isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict}).
3423	
3424	  Fix \(f \in R\). The sheaf condition for \(M\) over the finite cover \(\{D(r_j)\}\) and its pairwise
3425	  intersections presents the global sections as the finite equalizer
3426	  \[
3427	    \Gamma(M, \top) \;=\; \mathrm{eq}\Bigl(\textstyle\prod_j \Gamma(M, D(r_j))
3428	      \;\rightrightarrows\; \prod_{j,k} \Gamma(M, D(r_jr_k))\Bigr),
3429	  \]
3430	  the Mayer--Vietoris / unique-gluing description of sections
3431	  (\cref{lem:isLimitPullbackCone_mathlib}, \cref{lem:existsUnique_gluing_mathlib}); the same holds
3432	  with \(\top\) replaced by \(D(f)\) and the cover by \(\{D(fr_j)\}\). Localizing at
3433	  \(\mathrm{powers}\,f\) is exact and commutes with this finite limit because localization is flat
3434	  (\cref{lem:isLocalization_flat_mathlib}). On each cover piece the localization of
3435	  \(\Gamma(M, D(r_j))\) away from \(f\) is \(\Gamma(M, D(fr_j)) = \Gamma(M|_{D(r_j)}, D(f))\), a
3436	  localization of the tilde \(\widetilde{N_j}\) computed by
3437	  \cref{lem:isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict}; the same on the overlaps. Hence the localized equalizer
3438	  computes \(\Gamma(M, D(f))\), and the comparison map
3439	  \((\mathrm{powers}\,f)^{-1}\,\Gamma(M, \top) \to \Gamma(M, D(f))\) is an isomorphism: the section
3440	  restriction is \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}(\mathrm{powers}\,f)\) over \(R\). This is the
3441	  \(\Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})_f \cong \Gamma(X_f, \mathcal{F})\) of Stacks
3442	  \texttt{lemma-invert-f-sections} on \(X = \Spec R\), \(X_f = D(f)\), obtained directly from
3443	  local-tilde data without appeal to the global affine equivalence.
3444	\end{proof}

3446	\begin{lemma}[Quasi-coherent on an affine has trivial $\widetilde{(-)}$-counit (gap1)]

3452	  % assembly is now a one-liner: the genuine content lives in the section-localization descent
3453	  % (\cref{lem:section_localization_descent}, keystone D), which is fed into the in-file equivalence

3457	  % Route: analogies/quot-gap1-transport.md.
3458	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Schemes", Lemma \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine}
3459	  % (read from references/stacks-schemes.tex, L1279--1284).
3460	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $(X, \mathcal{O}_X) = (\Spec(R), \mathcal{O}_{\Spec(R)})$

3464	  \textit{The affine equivalence QCoh\((\Spec R)\) \(\simeq\) Mod\((R)\): a quasi-coherent module
3465	  sheaf on \(\Spec R\) is \(\widetilde{\Gamma(M)}\) up to canonical isomorphism.}
3466	  Let \(M\) be a sheaf of modules on \(\Spec R\) that is quasi-coherent
3467	  (\(\mathrm{IsQuasicoherent}\,M\)). Then the counit \(M.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}\) is an isomorphism;
3468	  equivalently \(M\) lies in the essential image of \(\widetilde{(-)} : \mathrm{Mod}(R) \to (\Spec
3469	  R).\mathrm{Modules}\).
3470	\end{lemma}
3471	% NOTE: the proof is a one-line assembly — feed the keystone descent (D) into the in-file
3472	% equivalence isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizedModule_restrict — so it routes through no transcribed
3473	% Stacks gluing proof; the SOURCE QUOTE above cites the lemma STATEMENT (Stacks
3474	% lemma-quasi-coherent-affine).
3475	\begin{proof}
3476	  \uses{lem:section_localization_descent, lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizedModule_restrict}
3477	  Since \(M\) is quasi-coherent, the section-localization descent
3478	  (\cref{lem:section_localization_descent}) gives that for every \(f \in R\) the section restriction
3479	  \(\Gamma(M, \top) \to \Gamma(M, D(f))\) is \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}(\mathrm{powers}\,f)\) over
3480	  \(R\). This is exactly the right-hand side of the equivalence
3481	  \cref{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizedModule_restrict} (already established in-file), whose
3482	  left-hand side is \(\mathrm{IsIso}\,M.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}\). Hence the counit is an isomorphism and
3483	  \(M\) is identified with \(\widetilde{\Gamma(M, \top)}\), placing \(M\) in the essential image of
3484	  \(\widetilde{(-)}\).
3485	\end{proof}

3487	\subsection{G1-assemble: from per-basic-open section localization to the
3488	\texorpdfstring{$\widetilde{(-)}$}{tilde}-counit}
3489	
3490	This subsection records the bridge \(\text{section-localization on every basic open} \Rightarrow
3491	\mathrm{IsIso}\,M.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}\): given that the section restriction localizes on every basic
3492	open, the tilde-Gamma counit is an isomorphism. Together with its converse
3493	\cref{lem:isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ} it assembles the equivalence
3494	\cref{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizedModule_restrict}, which is exactly what makes G1-core
3495	(\cref{lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization}) and gap1
3496	(\cref{lem:qcoh_affine_isIso_fromTildeΓ}) interderivable -- the route by which G1-core is obtained as
3497	a corollary of gap1. The supporting Mathlib facts are recorded as dependency anchors.
3498

3556	  localization of \(M\) at \(S\). If a linear map \(h : M' \to M''\) satisfies \(h \circ g = g'\),

3592	\begin{lemma}\leanok
3593	[$\mathrm{IsIso}\,M.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}$ from per-basic-open section localization
3594	(G1-assemble)]
3595	  \label{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isLocalizedModule_restrict}
3596	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isLocalizedModule_restrict}
3597	  \uses{lem:isIso_sheaf_of_isIso_app_basicOpen, lem:bijective_comp_of_localizations,
3598	    lem:isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict}
3599	  \textit{The G1-assemble reduction: section-localization on every basic open forces the
3600	  \(\widetilde{(-)}\)-counit to be an isomorphism.}
3601	  Let \(M\) be a sheaf of modules on \(\Spec R\), and suppose that for every \(f \in R\) the section
3602	  restriction \(\Gamma(M, \top) \to \Gamma(M, D(f))\) exhibits the target as
3603	  \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}(\mathrm{powers}\,f)\) over \(R\) -- exactly the conclusion of G1-core
3604	  (\cref{lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization}). Then the counit \(M.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}\) is an
3605	  isomorphism.
3606	\end{lemma}
3607	\begin{proof}
3608	  \uses{lem:isIso_sheaf_of_isIso_app_basicOpen, lem:bijective_comp_of_localizations,
3609	    lem:isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict}
3610	  Write \(N = \Gamma(M, \top)\). On each basic open \(D(f)\), the component of
3611	  \(\mathrm{modulesSpecToSheaf}(M.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ})\) is a map between two localizations of \(N\)
3612	  at \(\mathrm{powers}\,f\): the source \(\Gamma(\widetilde N, D(f))\) localizes \(N\) via the affine
3613	  tilde map (\cref{lem:isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict}) and the target \(\Gamma(M, D(f))\)
3614	  localizes \(N\) by hypothesis, the two being intertwined by the compatibility
3615	  \(\mathrm{toOpen\_fromTildeΓ\_app}\). Hence the component is bijective
3616	  (\cref{lem:bijective_comp_of_localizations}), so an isomorphism. By
3617	  \cref{lem:isIso_sheaf_of_isIso_app_basicOpen} the sheaf morphism
3618	  \(\mathrm{modulesSpecToSheaf}(M.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ})\) is then an isomorphism, and since
3619	  \(\mathrm{modulesSpecToSheaf}\) is fully faithful it reflects isomorphisms, giving
3620	  \(\mathrm{IsIso}\,M.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}\).
3621	\end{proof}

3690	\begin{lemma}[Properness as a morphism property]
3691	  \label{lem:isProper_mathlib}
3692	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.IsProper}
3693	  \mathlibok
3694	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
3695	  (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Proper}).}
3696	  A morphism \(f : X \to S\) of schemes is \emph{proper} iff it is separated,
3697	  universally closed, and locally of finite type. Properness is stable under base
3698	  change: if \(f : X \to S\) is proper and \(g : T \to S\) is any morphism then
3699	  the base change \(X \times_S T \to T\) is proper. Consequently the base-change
3700	  clause that Nitsure invokes (``properness \ldots\ preserved by base-change'')
3701	  applies directly to the proper-support condition below.
3702	\end{lemma}

3761	\section{The Quot functor}
3762	\label{sec:quot_functor}
3763	
3764	\begin{definition}\leanok
3765	[Quot functor]
3766	  \label{def:quot_functor}
3767	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotFunctor}
3768	  \uses{def:hilbert_polynomial, def:has_proper_support}
3769	
3770	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "The Functors \(\Hilb_{X/S}\) and
3771	  % \(\Quot_{E/X/S}\)" (read from
3772	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L394-L433
3773	  % + L481-L498).
3774	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
3775	  % "Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme and let \(X\to S\) be a finite type
3776	  %  scheme over it. Let \(E\) be a coherent sheaf on \(X\).
3777	  %  Let \(Sch_S\) denote the category of all locally noetherian schemes
3778	  %  over \(S\). For any \(T\to S\) in \(Sch_S\), a \textbf{family of quotients of \(E\)
3779	  %  parametrised by \(T\)} will mean
3780	  %  a pair \((\F,q)\) consisting of
3781	  %
3782	  %  (i) a coherent sheaf \(\F\) on \(X_T = X\times_ST\)
3783	  %  such that the schematic support of \(\F\) is proper over \(T\)
3784	  %  and \(\F\) is flat over \(T\), together with
3785	  %
3786	  %  (ii) a surjective \({\mathcal O}_{X_T}\)-linear homomorphism of sheaves
3787	  %  \(q:E_T  \to \F\) where \(E_T\) is the pull-back of \(E\) under
3788	  %  the projection \(X_T \to X\).
3789	  %
3790	  %  Two such families \((\F,q)\) and \((\F,q)\) parametrised by \(T\) will
3791	  %  be regarded as equivalent if \(\ker(q) = \ker(q')\)),
3792	  %  and \(\langle \F, q \rangle\) will denote
3793	  %  an equivalence class. Then as properness and flatness are preserved
3794	  %  by base-change, and as tensor-product is right exact,
3795	  %  the pull-back of \(\langle \F, q \rangle\) under an \(S\)-morphism \(T'\to T\) is
3796	  %  well-defined, which gives
3797	  %  a set-valued contravariant functor
3798	  %  \(\Quot_{E/X/S} : Sch_S \to Sets\) under which
3799	  %  \(\)T\mapsto  \{\mbox{ All } \langle \F, q \rangle \mbox{ parametrised by }T\}\(\)
3800	  %  [...]
3801	  %  This shows that the functor \(\Quot_{E/X/S}\) naturally decomposes
3802	  %  as a co-product
3803	  %  \(\)\Quot_{E/X/S} = \coprod_{{\Phi}\in \Q[\lambda]}\, \Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}\(\)
3804	  %  where for any polynomial \({\Phi}\in \Q[\lambda]\), the functor
3805	  %  \(\Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}\) associates
3806	  %  to any \(T\) the set of all equivalence classes of
3807	  %  families \(\langle \F,q \rangle\) such that at each \(t\in T\) the
3808	  %  Hilbert polynomial of the restriction \(\F_t\), calculated
3809	  %  using the pull-back of \(L\), is \({\Phi}\)."
3810	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
3811	  Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme, \(\pi : X \to S\) a finite-type morphism,
3812	  \(L\) a line bundle on \(X\), \(E\) a coherent sheaf on \(X\), and
3813	  \(\Phi \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]\). The \emph{Quot functor with Hilbert
3814	  polynomial \(\Phi\)}
3815	  \[
3816	    \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S} : (\mathrm{Sch}/S)^{op} \longrightarrow \mathbf{Set}
3817	  \]
3818	  sends an \(S\)-scheme \(T \to S\) to the set of equivalence classes
3819	  \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle\) of pairs \((\mathcal{F}, q)\) where
3820	  \begin{enumerate}
3821	    \item \(\mathcal{F}\) is a coherent sheaf on \(X_T = X \times_S T\) whose
3822	      schematic support is proper over \(T\) (\cref{def:has_proper_support}) and
3823	      which is flat over \(T\);
3824	    \item \(q : E_T \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\) is a surjective
3825	      \(\mathcal{O}_{X_T}\)-linear homomorphism, where \(E_T\) is the pull-back
3826	      of \(E\) along \(X_T \to X\);
3827	    \item for every \(t \in T\) the Hilbert polynomial of \(\mathcal{F}|_{X_t}\)
3828	      with respect to \(L|_{X_t}\) (\cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}) equals
3829	      \(\Phi\).
3830	  \end{enumerate}
3831	  Two pairs \((\mathcal{F}, q)\) and \((\mathcal{F}', q')\) are equivalent iff
3832	  \(\ker(q) = \ker(q')\). On morphisms \(f : T' \to T\) over \(S\), the functor
3833	  acts by pulling back the quotient along \(X_{T'} \to X_T\) (well-defined
3834	  since tensor product is right-exact and preserves both flatness and
3835	  properness). The Hilbert scheme is the special case
3836	  \(\Hilb^{\Phi,L}_{X/S} := \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{\mathcal{O}_X/X/S}\).
3837	\end{definition}

3839	\section{The Grassmannian scheme}
3840	\label{sec:grassmannian_scheme}
3841	
3842	The Quot construction proceeds by embedding \(\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}\) as a
3843	locally closed subfunctor of a Grassmannian over \(S\). Since Mathlib carries
3844	no Grassmannian \emph{scheme} (only a finite-rank-quotient variety in
3845	linear algebra), we package the construction here.

3977	  associated linear system gives a closed embedding

3983	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: see the "Construction of Grassmannian" sub-section of
3984	% [Nitsure], \S 1 (references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex,
3985	% L773-L930), which gives the construction in five named steps
3986	% ("Construction by gluing together affine patches", "Separatedness",

3988	% verbatim text is ~150 lines; it is reproduced piecewise in the
3989	% SOURCE QUOTE comment of the theorem statement above.
3990	\begin{proof}
3991	  
3992	  Over \(S = \Spec \mathbb{Z}\) and \(V = \mathcal{O}_S^{\oplus r}\) the
3993	  Grassmannian \(\mathrm{Gr}(r, d)\) is constructed by gluing
3994	  \(\binom{r}{d}\) affine charts \(U^I = \Spec\mathbb{Z}[X^I]\) indexed by
3995	  size-\(d\) subsets \(I \subseteq \{1, \dots, r\}\). The chart \(U^I\)
3996	  carries the matrix \(X^I\) of size \(d \times r\) whose \(I\)-th \(d \times d\)
3997	  minor is the identity, with the remaining \(d(r-d)\) entries free
3998	  variables (so \(U^I \cong \mathbb{A}^{d(r-d)}_{\mathbb{Z}}\)). The
3999	  transition maps \(\theta_{I,J} : U^I \cap U^J \to U^J \cap U^I\) defined
4000	  by \(X^J \mapsto (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\) on the locus where \(\det X^I_J\) is
4001	  invertible satisfy the cocycle condition
4002	  \(\theta_{I,K} = \theta_{I,J} \circ \theta_{J,K}\). The glued
4003	  \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d) \to \Spec\mathbb{Z}\) is smooth of relative dimension
4004	  \(d(r-d)\). Separatedness is checked from the diagonal
4005	  \(\Delta_{I,J} \subset U^I \times U^J\) cut out by \(X^J_I X^I - X^J = 0\).
4006	  Properness follows from the valuative criterion for DVRs: given
4007	  \(\varphi : \Spec K \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) over a DVR \(R\) with fraction
4008	  field \(K\), factoring through some chart \(U^I\) via a ring map
4009	  \(f : \mathbb{Z}[X^I] \to K\), choose \(J\) minimising the valuation
4010	  \(\nu(f(P^I_J))\) of the minor; on the chart \(U^J\) all entries have
4011	  non-negative valuation, so \(f\) extends uniquely to \(\Spec R\).
4012	
4013	  The universal quotient \(u : \mathcal{O}_{\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)}^{\oplus r}
4014	  \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{U}\) is given on \(U^I\) by the matrix \(X^I\)
4015	  and glued by \(g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)^{-1} \in GL_d(U^I \cap U^J)\). The
4016	  determinant line bundle \(\det\mathcal{U}\) has transition functions
4017	  \(\det g_{I,J} = 1/P^I_J\); the global sections
4018	  \(\sigma_I \in \Gamma(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d), \det\mathcal{U})\) defined by
4019	  \(\sigma_I|_{U^J} = P^J_I\) form a base-point-free linear system separating
4020	  points over \(\Spec\mathbb{Z}\), giving an embedding into
4021	  \(\mathbb{P}^{\binom{r}{d}-1}_{\mathbb{Z}}\), which is closed by
4022	  properness. Representability of \(\mathrm{Grass}(r,d)\) is then verified
4023	  on the universal pair \((\mathrm{Gr}(r,d), u)\): a quotient
4024	  \(q : \mathcal{O}_T^{\oplus r} \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\) of
4025	  rank \(d\) on \(T\) is everywhere locally trivialised; on the locus where
4026	  the columns indexed by \(I\) are a basis the matrix of \(q\) has the form
4027	  \(X^I\), defining the classifying morphism \(T \to U^I \subset
4028	  \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\). Base change from \(\mathbb{Z}\) to a general noetherian
4029	  base \(S\) with a vector bundle \(V\) is direct: trivialise \(V\) on an open
4030	  cover and glue. For non-trivial \(V\) the same construction produces
4031	  \(\mathrm{Gr}_S(V, d)\) with Pl\"ucker embedding into
4032	  \(\mathbb{P}_S(\bigwedge^d V)\).
4033	\end{proof}

237	  there is a natural bijection

241	  \]

243	  \(\mathcal{A} \to g_*\mathcal{O}_T\) and \(g^*\mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{O}_T\)).
